mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines below 3.5% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and falling federal funds rates create stagflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to heightened uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 285 and FEDFUNDS < 0.1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals manufacturing sector weakness, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% in 3 months and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and real sales of automobiles indicate strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST > 1700 and RSAFS > 630000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP growth above 3% signals robust economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in payroll employment below 150,000新增 indicates labor market weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS < 150000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% combined with stable federal funds rates signals central bank inaction, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to eroding purchasing power.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 285 and FEDFUNDS < 0.1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.
